name: Chocolate Marshmallow Nut Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Use gluten-free ingredients for someone on a celiac diet.
2. Spray a 9-inch square cake pan with non-stick vegetable spray, or line the pan with waxed paper.
3. Melt chocolate chips and peanut butter in a large saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Alternatively, microwave at medium power for about 2 1/2 minutes until the mixture can be stirred smooth. Stir in corn syrup. Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes.
4. In a food processor, puree the cooked red kidney beans and ground cinnamon. Stir into the chocolate mixture until well blended.
5. Add marshmallows and chopped nuts to the mixture until coated. If mini marshmallows are not available, cut larger marshmallows with wet scissors.
6. Press the mixture evenly into the prepared pan. Chill for about 45 minutes or until firm. For best results, cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours or up to 6 days before serving.
7. Cut into 24 squares.
1 square contains approximately 4 calories, 1 fruit and vegetable choice, 1 fats and oils choice (5 grams total fat, 1 gram saturated fat), 0 cholesterol, 2 grams protein, 11 grams carbohydrate, 21 milligrams sodium, and 103 milligrams potassium.
